title: chamaecrista serpens var. wrightii
intro: Chamaecrista serpens var. wrightii, also known as Wright's sensitive plant, is a perennial legume native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Chamaecrista serpens var. wrightii include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and treating conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used topically to treat skin conditions or ingested to support overall health and well-being.
